excerpt: These two beings called the Emanators—those who emerge together—performed tawhīd by observing the First Intellect and following its example.
source_work: al-Rahat al-ʿAql or comparable foundational Ismaili metaphysical text
source_location_hint: discussion of al-Munbaʿithūn and their relationship to al-ʿAql al-Awwal

excerpt: The First Intellect performed tawhīd without exemplar, deriving it from its own nature; the Emanators advanced after witnessing the First Intellect.
source_location_hint: on the distinction between ibdāʿ and inbʿāth in the emanation doctrine

excerpt: From their initial creation in primary perfection until attaining secondary perfection, the Emanators remained in dormancy, observing the radiant light of the First Intellect before advancing toward tawhīd.
source_location_hint: on the states of existence, dormancy (durus), and emanation (inbʿāth)

excerpt: The essence of the First Intellect's development was the act of tawhīd toward its Originator; because of this action, a veil of radiant light was placed upon it.
source_work: al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama or Tanbih al-Hadi
source_location_hint: on the relationship between essence, manifestation, and the veil of light in spiritual development

excerpt: The Second Emanator became captivated by the outward radiance of the First Intellect rather than perceiving the inner act of tawhīd, thus falling into the illusion of equivalence and failing to attain secondary perfection.
source_location_hint: on the error of wham al-musāwāt (illusion of equivalence) and its consequences

excerpt: The First Intellect's testimony of tawhīd was to the Almighty; the Second Emanator's testimony, while formally the same, was to the First Intellect, who had become his ilāh.
source_location_hint: on the difference in the objects and significance of the testimonies of the First Two

excerpt: When the First Emanator performed tawhīd, twenty-seven others perceived his vision and attained secondary perfection without needing explicit invitation, as if they had witnessed through his witnessing.
source_location_hint: on the formation of the circle of twenty-eight and the principle of witnessing through witnessing

excerpt: The Sidra (lote-tree) stands uniquely in solitude with no other beside it, symbolizing the First Intellect in its singularity; the Garden of Refuge represents the First Emanator gathered with his circle of twenty-seven souls.
source_location_hint: on the cosmological significance of Sidrat al-Muntahā and Jannāt al-Maʾwā in the Ismaili metaphysical system

excerpt: In the prayer row, only the Imām and his deputy remain fixed while all other worshippers shift positions, creating an image of the spiritual world where only the First Intellect and First Emanator remain unchanging while all others evolve and merge into the realm of the First Emanator.
source_author: Hamid al-Din al-Kirmani or Qadi al-Nuʿman
source_location_hint: on the cosmological principle reflected in congregational prayer

excerpt: Alif is the symbol of the First Intellect, born without guide or teacher; Lam is the symbol of the Self, constituted from the First Intellect just as Lam is formed from the lines of Alif.
source_location_hint: on the symbolic and linguistic correspondence between the letters Alif and Lam and the First Intellect and First Emanator

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah said that Allah created the Pen and commanded it to write all that would occur until the Day of Resurrection; this is why it is called the Preserved Tablet. The Qur'ān is the utterance of the First Intellect, preserved by the First Emanator.
source_work: Hadith (transmitted through Islamic tradition) and taʾwīl thereof in Ismaili sources
source_location_hint: on the cosmological principle of the Pen, Stream, and Tablet

excerpt: The First Intellect is the pen of creation, inscribing the twenty-eight luminous gems of the Kalimah upon the First Emanator with the ink of Qudsi materials, bestowing all knowledge of what was and shall be.
source_author: Hamid al-Din al-Kirmani or Abu Yaʿqub al-Sijistani
source_location_hint: on the esoteric meaning of the Pen and Tablet and the transmission of sacred knowledge through the First Intellect and First Emanator
